what_it_does: This file is a return-to backlog/plan that lists the work needed to fully build and integrate the ledger's analysis/interpretive layer into the existing system, covering model serving, role-based interpretation, coverage auditing, flow registration, a Tim-facing front, and real data analysis, derived from 4B first-run findings.
observations: The resident 4B model runs on :8001 via a systemd vLLM service with tight GPU memory and a concurrency/sequence-length tradeoff.
There is registry drift between ops/services.json (AWQ on :8000) and the actual FP8 service on :8001.
A provider reload-after-loadout bug caused ConnectionRefused because the cognition engine cached a stale base_url across a model swap.
The interpret_file roles already exist; the bespoke ops/ledger_interpret_* producers should be retired in favor of running those roles through the engine.
The coverage_audit draft should be registered as a role and run via run_items over file addresses.
ledger_metadata, ledger_fact_edges, and ledger_changes are deterministic ops that should become registered flows/cascades.
Selective re-run / carry-forward (F6) is described as the unbuilt keystone for keeping the ledger current incrementally.
The Stage 1 front must render the ledger to Tim's recognition through tree/graph/filter views, state-as-colour, edge graph, and metadata.
The use case is not considered proven until Tim can actually see and use analyzed data, not just DB counts.
The standing notes warn of concurrent external upgrades, lean DB/IO, and retiring bespoke urllib model invocation.
standouts: The core principle: everything interpretive/audit/edge/metadata/drift/analysis must run as in-system roles/flows with a front, not bespoke side scripts.
Selective re-run / carry-forward (F6) is called the unbuilt keystone for maintaining an up-to-date ledger.
Proof is defined as Tim being able to see and direct using the analyzed data via the converged front.
conventions: Uses a sectioned backlog format (A-F) plus a standing summary.
References external findings with ticket-like IDs (F-1, F-9, F6, F2).
Mixes operational configuration details with architectural decisions and direct action items.
Breaks pure documentation convention by including warnings about concurrent external sessions and expected breakage.
concerns: GPU VRAM is near full, leaving little room to raise concurrency without trading sequence length.
The plan depends on an unbuilt selective re-run/carry-forward mechanism for incremental updates.
It warns that another session is concurrently upgrading the unit, so drift/breakage is expected.
The standing phrase 'No confidence (tags+counts)' is opaque without additional context.
notes: A build-and-integration backlog that maps how the ledger's interpretive layer should move from bespoke scripts into the system's role/flow/front surfaces so Tim can recognize and direct from analyzed data.
questions: What is the exact v2 interpretive schema and where is it defined?
What are the precise semantics and implementation of 'selective re-run / carry-forward' (F6)?
Who or what is the 'another session' upgrading the unit?
What does 'No confidence (tags+counts)' mean in the standing section?
What is the current completion status of sections A through F?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to note everything required for a full build and integration into the system, and the content is a structured backlog of specific technical gaps and tasks aligned with that purpose.
apparent_themes: Recognition-driven front and proving value to Tim
Root-cause fixing as issues are encountered
intention_signals: It is trying to converge the ledger's analytical capabilities into the main system so they are routable, reusable, observable, and usable by Tim, while fixing root causes discovered during the 4B first run.
novelty: It explicitly frames the interpretive layer as in-system roles/flows rather than external producer scripts, and makes Tim's recognition through a front the criterion for proving the use case.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete integration backlog that ties model-serving constraints, role-based interpretation, coverage auditing, flow registration, front rendering, and incremental update semantics into one coordination artifact; a fused system would keep it as the plan for building the ledger's system-native analytical surface.
summary_for_embedding: A build-and-integration backlog for moving the ledger's interpretive, audit, edge, metadata, and drift capabilities into the system's role/flow/front surfaces, derived from 4B first-run findings.
intention_for_embedding: To enumerate and coordinate the work needed to make the ledger's analysis layer run natively within the system and prove its value through a Tim-facing front.
